Adrenocorticotropic Hormone (ACTH) 
URL of this page: https://medlineplus.gov/lab-tests/adrenocorticotropic-hormone-acth/ 
Adrenocorticotropic Hormone (ACTH) 
To use the sharing features on this page, please enable JavaScript. 
What is an adrenocorticotropic hormone (ACTH) test? 
This test measures the level of adrenocorticotropic hormone (ACTH) in a sample of your blood. A hormone is a chemical messenger in your bloodstream that controls the actions of certain cells or organs. ACTH tells your adrenal glands, two small glands that sit above
